27.reason: [Error: sizeof(tree) allocates memory for a pointer, not the struct node. Should be sizeof(struct node) or sizeof(**T)]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: sizeof(tree) allocates memory for a pointer, not the struct node. Should be sizeof(struct node) or sizeof(**T)]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: sizeof(tree) is the size of a pointer, not the struct. Should be sizeof(struct node) to allocate the correct memory size.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: malloc uses sizeof(tree) which is size of pointer, not size of struct node. Should be sizeof(struct node) or sizeof(**T).]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  error: malloc allocates size of pointer, not size of struct node; should be sizeof(struct node)
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  sizeof(tree) returns the size of a pointer, not the struct node. This causes insufficient memory allocation and leads to undefined behavior. Should be sizeof(struct node) or sizeof(**T).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  sizeof(tree) calculates the size of a pointer type, not the struct node. It should be sizeof(struct node) or sizeof(**T).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrect allocation size: sizeof(tree) is the size of a pointer, not the struct node. Should be sizeof(struct node).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrect memory allocation: sizeof(tree) is the size of a pointer, not the struct node. Should be sizeof(struct node).]

33.reason: [Error: Condition reversed. Should be if(dat < (*T)->data) then go left, else go right. This puts equal values to left instead of right.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Condition reversed. Should be if(dat < (*T)->data) then go left, else go right. This puts equal values to left instead of right.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  error: condition should be dat < (*T)->data to ensure left subtree contains values less than root and right subtree contains values greater than or equal to root
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  This condition incorrectly sends equal values to the left child. The problem states that the right child's value must be greater than or equal to the root, so equal values should go right. Should use dat < (*T)->data for left, or (*T)->data <= dat for right.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  This condition sends equal values to the left child, violating the requirement that the right child must be greater than or equal to the root. It should be (*T)->data <= dat.
